mnemonics.title: Mobile IP Components
mnemonics.content: Remember the three Mobile IP agents as 'My Home Has Foreigners' - MN (Mobile Node), HA (Home Agent), FA (Foreign Agent)

mnemonics.title: Mobile IP Registration Steps
mnemonics.content: Use 'DARF' - Discover agents, Advertise, Register with HA, Forward packets through tunnel

acronyms.usage: Temporary IP address assigned to mobile node in foreign network for packet delivery
Router in mobile node's home network that maintains permanent address and tunnels packets
Router in visited network that provides CoA and assists mobile node connectivity
The mobile device that moves across different networks while maintaining connectivity
When a mobile node moves from one foreign network to another
When mobile node returns to home network, HA functions continue but with local care-of address

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a tunnel (CoA pathway) connecting Home Network to Foreign Network, with packets inside representing letters being mailed through an underground pipe rather than going around
Draw a triangle with CN (Correspondent), HA (Home), MN (Mobile) - notice packets from CN to MN must go through HA making a triangle path instead of direct line

analogies.analogy: Like a post office that holds your mail (packets) while you're traveling and forwards it to your temporary address (CoA)
Like a hotel address where you receive mail while traveling, different from your permanent home address
Like a secret passage - the outer envelope (new header) carries the original message through networks while hiding its true destination
